dialogues.text: Let's warm up your graph skills. Here's a bar graph. Check the scale. How many students chose soccer?
12 students. Scale of 2. Each mark on the axis shows 2 students.
Now a question about this data. How many more students chose soccer than tennis?
6 more. How many more means compare. We subtract. Soccer is 12, tennis is 6, difference is 6.
Different graph, different scale. Quick check.
You've got graph reading down. You've got comparison down. But look at THIS question.
How many more students chose pizza than tacos and salad together?
See that word 'together'? This question needs more than one step. We'll come back to THIS question in the lesson. And you'll be able to answer it.
